citations: [Beyond TG‑43: A PRISMA-based systematic review on model-based dose-calculation algorithms in brachytherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/42159092/)
[The dosimetric impact of replacing the TG-43 algorithm by model based dose calculation for liver brachytherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32151255/)
[Dosimetric impact of applying a model-based dose calculation algorithm for skin cancer brachytherapy (interventional radiotherapy).](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38230401/)
[A generic TG-186 shielded applicator for commissioning model-based dose calculation algorithms for high-dose-rate (192) Ir brachytherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28722180/)
[A MC-based anthropomorphic test case for commissioning model-based dose calculation in interstitial breast 192-Ir HDR brachytherapy.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37194638/)

evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 42159092. 2026/05/01 00:00. PURPOSE: The AAPM TG-43 formalism has long served as the clinical 
standard for brachytherapy dose calculation but assumes a homogeneous water 
equivalent medium
evidence.source_title: Beyond TG‑43: A PRISMA-based systematic review on model-based dose-calculation algorithms in brachytherapy.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 42159092. 2026/05/01 00:00. PURPOSE: The AAPM TG-43 formalism has long served as the clinical 
standard for brachytherapy dose calculation but assumes a homogeneous water 
equivalent medium, overlooking limited scattering conditions and tissue 
heterogeneities. Model-based dose-calculation algorithms (MBDCAs), including 
Monte Carlo (MC) simulations overcome these limitations by accounting for real 
tissue composition, scatter, and applicator attenuation. This systematic review 
evalu
